title: Grokking Algorithms
subtitle: An Illustrated Guide for Programmers and Other Curious People
author: Aditya Y. Bhargava
themes: Algorithmic thinking as a visual, intuitive process
Just-in-time teaching: concepts only when you need them
The power of illustrations to make abstract ideas concrete
Tradeoffs between time, space, and simplicity
NP-completeness and the limits of computation
excerpt: An algorithm is a set of instructions for accomplishing a task. Every piece of code could be called an algorithm, but this book covers the more interesting bits. I chose the algorithms in this book for inclusion because they're fast, or they solve interesting problems, or both. In each case, I'll describe the algorithm, give you an example, talk about the running time in Big O notation, and explore what other types of problems could be solved by the same algorithm.
